message:
fix(win32): resolve cross-compile issues and provision mingw-w64 validation

Makefile: add -DASX_BUILDING_DLL to CFLAGS for the build-win32 target so
the DLL export macro is defined when building with x86_64-w64-mingw32-gcc.
Without it, symbols were not exported and the link step failed silently.

src/platform/win32/hooks.c: correct the include-order comment to reflect
that winsock2.h must precede windows.h (not the other way around) while
bcrypt.h still needs Windows types already in scope. The previous comment
was misleading and contradicted the actual header ordering required by
the MinGW toolchain.

.beads/issues.jsonl: close two Win32 toolchain tracking issues:
- bd-vp0w: provision mingw-w64 locally and validate build-win32 (primary
  follow-up bead; closed after successful cross-compile evidence).
- bd-6guk: duplicate of bd-vp0w covering the same cross-compile environment
  gap; closed as duplicate with reference to the preexisting visible issue.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id": "bd-66l.7", "title": "Implement anti-butchering proof-block gate for semantic-sensitive changes", "description": "Background:
Plan section 6.13 requires semantic-sensitive pull requests to include guarantee-substitution notes, proof artifacts, and fallback/isomorphism statements.

Subtasks:
- Define required metadata template for semantic-sensitive changes.
- Implement CI check that fails when required proof fields are missing.
- Link required fields to traceability index and artifact locations.
- Document how to request and review exceptions.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Semantic-sensitive changes cannot merge without complete proof block metadata.
- Metadata links are machine-checkable and reviewer-friendly.
- Exception flow is explicit and auditable.", "notes": "Verified anti-butchering proof-block enforcement is wired and auditable: Makefile lint-anti-butchering target invokes tools/ci/check_anti_butchering.sh; CI check job runs lint-anti-butchering and records manifest rerun pointers. Gate fail-path validated locally (no metadata -> exit 1 with missing fields and anti_butcher JSON artifact). Gate pass-path validated with complete Guarantee Impact metadata block (exit 0, artifact emitted). Added compatibility shim tools/ci/check_proof_block_gate.sh -> check_anti_butchering.sh and updated GUARANTEE_SUBSTITUTION_MATRIX exception flow docs with explicit non-zero budget review steps and rerun guidance.", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "assignee": "LilacHawk", "created_at": "2026-02-27T00:57:19.340855881Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-02-28T03:44:09.208410249Z", "closed_at": "2026-02-28T03:39:00.921139480Z", "close_reason": "done", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["ci", "governance", "quality-gates"], "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "bd-66l.7", "depends_on_id": "bd-296.6", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-03-02T04:37:11Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-66l.7", "depends_on_id": "bd-296.8", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-03-02T04:37:11Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-66l.7", "depends_on_id": "bd-66l", "type": "parent-child", "created_at": "2026-03-02T04:37:11Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-66l.7", "depends_on_id": "bd-66l.6", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-03-02T04:37:11Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}]}
 {"id": "bd-66l.8", "title": "Implement per-target binary-size and cold-start SLO gates", "description": "Background:
Idea round 2 includes per-target binary size and cold-start gates as embedded adoption multipliers.

Subtasks:
- Capture size and cold-start baselines for key target profiles and triplets.
- Define budget policy and tolerance windows per profile/resource class.
- Integrate checks into CI with clear delta reporting and reproducible rerun commands.
- Emit structured gate artifacts (target, baseline, delta, suspect modules, run metadata) for trend analysis and release review.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Regressions in size/start metrics fail gate when outside approved budgets.
- Reports identify changed targets and likely contributing modules.
- Baseline updates require explicit rationale and audit trail.
- Gate artifacts are machine-readable and linkable from release evidence bundles.", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-02-27T00:57:19.423164995Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-03-01T05:29:18.543969085Z", "closed_at": "2026-03-01T05:29:18.543951361Z", "close_reason": "done", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["ci", "embedded", "performance"], "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "bd-66l.8", "depends_on_id": "bd-1md.6", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-03-02T04:37:11Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-66l.8", "depends_on_id": "bd-66l", "type": "parent-child", "created_at": "2026-03-02T04:37:11Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-66l.8", "depends_on_id": "bd-66l.5", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-03-02T04:37:11Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "bd-66l.8", "depends_on_id": "bd-ix8.4", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-03-02T04:37:11Z", "created_by": "import", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}]}
 {"id": "bd-66l.9", "title": "Enforce per-bead evidence linkage contract for unit/e2e/logging completeness", "description": "Background:
The hard-part verification rules require every semantic-sensitive implementation bead to carry explicit proof linkage, not only CI-level gating.

Subtasks:
- Define required evidence fields per implementation bead: linked unit tests, linked e2e scripts, expected artifact manifests, and replay/parity references.
- Require linkage against the unit/invariant completeness matrix (bd-1md.12) and canonical e2e suite contract (bd-1md.10) so evidence remains coverage-complete, not ad hoc.
- Add validation tooling that checks bead metadata and notes for required evidence references before status transitions to done.
- Integrate checks with anti-butchering proof-block gate and traceability index updates.
- Provide reviewer/operator guidance for interpreting evidence packs quickly.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Semantic-sensitive beads cannot be marked complete without linked unit/e2e/logging evidence.
- Evidence packs reference explicit unit/invariant obligations and at least one canonical e2e lane per covered semantic surface.
- Validation failures are explicit and actionable.
- Evidence links remain machine-checkable for audit and release workflows.", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "assignee": "CoralValley", "created_at":  {"id":"bd-6u8q","title":"Child regions: Implement real Q2 check in quiescence.c","description":"Replace the hardcoded Q2 conjunct at src/runtime/quiescence.c line 150:

BEFORE:
    /* Q2: all child regions closed (walking skeleton: always true) */
    out->q2_children_closed = 1;

AFTER:
    /* Q2: all child regions closed */
    out->q2_children_closed = (r->child_count == 0) ? 1 : 0;

This is a one-line semantic change but it's the core fix for the quiescence contract violation. With child_count properly maintained by the open_child/close tracking, this correctly reflects whether all children have reached CLOSED state.

## Files
- src/runtime/quiescence.c (modify: line 150)","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T13:59:44.069290149Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T20:31:02.603644945Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T20:31:02.603296349Z","close_reason":"Implemented real Q2 child-closure check in detailed quiescence report","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-6u8q","depends_on_id":"bd-877h","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T14:01:47.466979962Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

@@ -420,6 +421,7 @@
 {"id": "bd-va8n.2", "title": "Create .clang-tidy config and install clang-tidy locally", "description": "## Context
clang-tidy provides static analysis beyond what the compiler warns about. The CI
pipeline runs it as the lint gate, but locally it reports SKIP.

**CRITICAL FINDING:** .clang-tidy DOES NOT EXIST in this repository. It is not
merely missing from the local toolchain — the configuration file itself has never
been created. This bead must CREATE it, not just verify it.

## Background
Without .clang-tidy, clang-tidy runs with default checks, which produces too many
false positives on C99 code. A well-tuned .clang-tidy file enables useful checks
while suppressing irrelevant ones.

## Task
1. **Create .clang-tidy** in the repository root with C99-appropriate checks:
   - Enable: bugprone-*, cert-*, clang-analyzer-*, readability-implicit-bool-conversion
   - Disable: modernize-* (C++ only), cppcoreguidelines-* (C++ only),
     readability-magic-numbers (too noisy for embedded constants),
     bugprone-easily-swappable-parameters (too noisy for callback-heavy API)
   - Configure WarningsAsErrors for critical checks (null-dereference, use-after-free)
   - Add HeaderFilterRegex to only check project headers (not system headers)
   - Document rationale for each enabled/disabled check group
2. Install clang-tidy (version >= 17) on the local build host
3. Run clang-tidy on all source files with the project configuration
4. Triage findings:
   - Fix real bugs immediately
   - Suppress false positives with NOLINT + explanation comment
   - Adjust check configuration to reduce noise without hiding real issues
5. Verify make lint passes locally (no longer reports SKIP)

## Check Selection Methodology
- Start with a conservative set (bugprone-*, cert-*)
- Run on full codebase, categorize all findings
- If a check produces >10 false positives and <2 real bugs, disable it
- Document the triage in the .clang-tidy file as comments

## Acceptance Criteria
- .clang-tidy exists in repository root and is committed
- clang-tidy --version reports >= 17
- make lint exits 0 (PASS, not SKIP)
- Zero un-triaged findings
- Each disabled check has a documented rationale
- NOLINT annotations include explanation (e.g., // NOLINT(bugprone-foo): reason)

## Testing
- Run: clang-tidy src/**/*.c -- -Iinclude/ -std=c99
- Run: make lint
- Verify zero warnings/errors on clean code
- Intentionally introduce a bug (null deref) and verify clang-tidy catches it
- Remove the intentional bug after verification

## Why This Matters
Static analysis catches bugs that tests miss (especially UB, null derefs, type
confusion). Creating .clang-tidy with tuned checks gives meaningful results
instead of a wall of false positives.", "status": "closed", "priority": 3, "issue_type": "task", "estimated_minutes": 60, "created_at": "2026-03-12T17:16:20.496825071Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-03-13T01:36:57.301315974Z", "closed_at": "2026-03-13T01:36:57.299330107Z", "close_reason": "done. Evidence: tests/unit/core/test_config_types.c. Additional evidence: tests/e2e/e2e_core_lifecycle.c, tests/e2e/e2e_foundational_contracts.c, tests/invariant/lifecycle/test_lifecycle_legality.c; fixtures/rust_reference/, build/conformance/, .beads/issues.jsonl", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "bd-va8n.2", "depends_on_id": "bd-va8n", "type": "parent-child", "created_at": "2026-03-12T17:16:20.496825071Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}]}
 {"id":"bd-vhzw","title":"POSIX: Implement epoll reactor hook","description":"Implement POSIX reactor hook using epoll in src/platform/posix/hooks.c.

## What to implement
A reactor context struct holding epoll fd and registration tracking. Three functions:
1. posix_reactor_init(): epoll_create1(EPOLL_CLOEXEC), allocate context
2. posix_reactor_wait(void *ctx, uint32_t timeout_ms, uint32_t *ready_count): epoll_wait wrapper. Returns ASX_OK with ready_count set.
3. posix_reactor_register/deregister: epoll_ctl ADD/DEL for fd tracking

The hook signature for wait is: asx_status (*asx_reactor_wait_fn)(void *ctx, uint32_t timeout_ms, uint32_t *out_ready_count).

## Dependencies
Depends on include/asx/platform/atomics.h (already implemented) for any fence operations in reactor event processing.

## Portability
epoll is Linux-only. For macOS/BSD, use kqueue. Recommend #ifdef __linux__ for epoll, else kqueue fallback. Can start with epoll-only and add kqueue later.

## Testing
- Create epoll reactor, register a pipe fd, write to pipe, verify reactor reports 1 ready
- Test timeout behavior (0ms returns immediately, short timeout returns on time)

## Files
- src/platform/posix/hooks.c (implement)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T13:58:32.453719673Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-04T01:30:50.729730083Z","closed_at":"2026-04-04T01:30:50.729365653Z","close_reason":"Implemented POSIX epoll reactor hook in src/platform/posix/hooks.c: posix_reactor_wait() using epoll_create1/epoll_wait on Linux with poll(2) fallback on other POSIX systems, posix_ghost_reactor_wait() for deterministic mode. Wired into asx_posix_hooks_install(). Lazy-initializes epoll fd on first wait. Handles EINTR gracefully. Unit test evidence: compiles clean with -DASX_PROFILE_POSIX -Werror on Linux. E2e evidence: tests/e2e/e2e_core_lifecycle.c. Artifact evidence: build/","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

@@ -22,7 +22,7 @@
 #include <stdio.h>
 #include <string.h>
 
-/* windows.h must come before winsock2.h on some toolchains */
